fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nebraska Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nebraska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity nebraska, how to transfer occupational therapist license to nebraska, occupational therapist compact nebraska, nebraska occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Nebraska DHHS — Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
board.mailing_address: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Public Health, Licensure Unit, P.O. Box 94986, Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Nebraska.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Access the NE DHHS Online Licensing Portal and create a new account if you do not already have one. Once logged in, initiate an 'Initial Licensure Application' specifically for Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the comprehensive online application form with all required personal, educational, and professional details. Submit the non-refundable application fee of $110.00 directly through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your occupational therapy program to be sent directly from your educational institution. Additionally, ensure your official NBCOT (National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy) score report is sent directly from NBCOT to the Nebraska Credentialing Division.

steps.title: Complete Nebraska Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Access and successfully complete the Nebraska Occupational Therapy Jurisprudence Examination. This exam assesses knowledge of state laws and regulations governing OT practice and is typically guided or administered through the online application process.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Licensure (if applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have previously held an occupational therapy license in another state or jurisdiction, you must arrange for official verification of your licensure status to be sent directly from each respective licensing board to Nebraska.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log into your NE DHHS Online Licensing Portal account to track the progress of your application. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Credentialing Division to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Nebraska offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Nebraska DHHS — Occupational Therapy for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Nebraska is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Nebraska without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Nebraska also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Nebraska OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $175. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Nebraska have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Nebraska has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Nebraska is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Nebraska without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Nebraska also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Nebraska
hero.dek: Occupational Therapists wishing to practice in Nebraska must apply for initial licensure through the NE DHHS Online Licensing Portal, managed by the Credentialing Division of the Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the portal URL, click 'Need to create an account? Click here.' Provide your full legal name, date of birth, and email address. An email will be sent to confirm your email address. Once confirmed, you can proceed with creating a password and security questions to finalize your account setup.
